nws_forecast.name: Tonight
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Mostly Cloudy then Chance Rain Showers
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain showers after 5am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 37. Southeast wind 10 to 14 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Monday
nws_forecast.detailed: Rain showers and patchy fog before 11am, then patchy fog and showers and thunderstorms. Some of the storms could produce heavy rain. Cloudy, with a high near 60. Southeast wind around 16 mph, with gusts as high as 32 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between three quarters and one inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Monday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Showers and thunderstorms before 8pm, then rain showers and patchy fog. Some of the storms could produce heavy rain. Cloudy. Low around 39, with temperatures rising to around 50 overnight. South wind 15 to 20 mph, with gusts as high as 60 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between a half and three quarters of an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Tuesday
nws_forecast.detailed: Rain showers and patchy fog before 8am. Mostly sunny, with a high near 46. Southwest wind around 16 mph, with gusts as high as 33 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Tuesday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly clear, with a low around 23. West wind around 14 mph, with gusts as high as 29 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Wednesday
nws_forecast.detailed: Sunny, with a high near 38. West wind 6 to 12 mph, with gusts as high as 22 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Wednesday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 29. South wind around 8 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Thursday
nws_forecast.detailed: Partly sunny, with a high near 43. South wind 7 to 10 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Thursday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 32. South wind around 9 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Friday
nws_forecast.detailed: Partly sunny, with a high near 49. Southwest wind around 9 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Friday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain after 7pm. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 34. Southwest wind around 10 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain. Partly sunny, with a high near 48. South wind around 13 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain before 3am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 31. Southwest wind around 14 mph, with gusts as high as 24 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%.

nws_forecast.name: Sunday
nws_forecast.detailed: A slight chance of rain between 8am and 11am, then a slight chance of rain and snow. Mostly sunny, with a high near 44. West wind around 15 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph.

nws_alerts.areaDesc: Central Middlesex County; Western Essex; Eastern Essex; Western Norfolk; Southeast Middlesex; Suffolk; Eastern Norfolk; Northern Bristol; Western Plymouth; Eastern Plymouth; Southern Bristol; Southern Plymouth; Barnstable; Dukes; Nantucket; Northwest Providence; Southeast Providence; Western Kent; Eastern Kent; Bristol; Washington; Newport; Block Island
nws_alerts.headline: High Wind Warning issued March 16 at 12:11AM EDT until March 17 at 6:00AM EDT by NWS Boston/Norton MA
nws_alerts.description:
* WHAT...South winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Portions of eastern, northeastern, and southeastern
Massachusetts and northern and southern Rhode Island.

* WHEN...From 5 PM this afternoon to 6 AM EDT Tuesday.

* IMPACTS...Damaging winds will blow down trees and power lines.
Power outages are possible. Travel will be difficult, especially
for high profile vehicles.
nws_alerts.instruction:
Remain in the lower levels of your home during the windstorm, and
avoid windows. Watch for falling debris and tree limbs. Use caution
if you must drive.

NWSheadline: HIGH WIND WARNING IN EFFECT FROM 5 PM THIS AFTERNOON TO 6 AM EDT TUESDAY

nws_alerts.areaDesc: Hartford; Tolland; Windham; Western Franklin; Eastern Franklin; Northern Worcester; Central Middlesex County; Western Essex; Eastern Essex; Western Hampshire; Western Hampden; Eastern Hampshire; Eastern Hampden; Southern Worcester; Western Norfolk; Southeast Middlesex; Suffolk; Eastern Norfolk; Northern Bristol; Western Plymouth; Eastern Plymouth; Southern Bristol; Southern Plymouth; Barnstable; Northwest Middlesex County; Northwest Providence; Southeast Providence; Western Kent; Eastern Kent; Bristol; Washington; Newport
nws_alerts.headline: Flood Watch issued March 15 at 12:14PM EDT until March 17 at 8:00AM EDT by NWS Boston/Norton MA
nws_alerts.description:
Heavy rain may fall on a deep primed snowpack leading to the melt
increasing. Flows in rivers may increase quickly and reach critical
levels.

* WHAT...Flooding caused by rain and snowmelt is possible.

* WHERE...Portions of northern Connecticut, including the following
areas, Hartford CT, Tolland CT and Windham CT, Massachusetts,
including the following areas, Barnstable MA, Central Middlesex
MA, Eastern Essex MA, Eastern Franklin MA, Eastern Hampden MA,
Eastern Hampshire MA, Eastern Norfolk MA, Eastern Plymouth MA,
Northern Bristol MA, Northern Middlesex MA, Northern Worcester MA,
Southeast Middlesex MA, Southern Bristol MA, Southern Plymouth MA,
Southern Worcester MA, Suffolk MA, Western Essex MA, Western
Franklin MA, Western Hampden MA, Western Hampshire MA, Western
Norfolk MA and Western Plymouth MA, and Rhode Island, including
the following areas, Bristol RI, Eastern Kent RI, Newport RI,
Northwest Providence RI, Southeast Providence RI, Washington RI
and Western Kent RI.

* WHEN...From late tonight through Tuesday morning.

* IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of rivers,
creeks, streams, and other low-lying and flood-prone locations.
Flooding may occur in poor drainage and urban areas. Area creeks
and streams are running high and could flood with more heavy rain.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
nws_alerts.instruction:
You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood
Warnings. Those living in areas prone to flooding should be prepared
to take action should flooding develop.

NWSheadline: FLOOD WATCH IN EFFECT FROM LATE TONIGHT THROUGH TUESDAY MORNING
